name: usbethernet
description: USB-C Ethernet Adapters for Wired Streaming

blocks.text: USB-C Ethernet Adapters

A USB-C → Ethernet adapter plugged into your headset gives it a wired connection to your network. This is the most stable, lowest-latency option for PCVR streaming (Virtual Desktop / Steam Link) because it removes WiFi interference entirely.

**Key rule:** Adapters with a **Realtek** chipset work; **ASIX** chipsets do **not** (they drop the connection when streaming starts). Get the version **with Power Delivery (PD) charging** so the headset charges while you play.

Select an option below for details.
USB-C Ethernet Adapters - Overview

Use a Realtek-based USB-C Ethernet adapter (with PD charging) to put your headset on a wired LAN with your PC.

**Highly Recommended**
BENFEI USB-C Gigabit (PD) - most-vouched, ~$15-20
MyGatti Gold (100W PD) - cleanest single-cable setup

**Recommended**
Cable Matters 2.5GbE (100W PD) - the canonical pick
Cable Matters 140W charging / generic 2.5GbE w/ PD

**Budget**
Benfei/Ruxely ~$10 (60W PD, 1GbE)

Select a product from the dropdown for details and links.

blocks.name: Chipset rule
blocks.value:
__Works__: Realtek RTL8153 (1GbE), RTL8156/8156BG (2.5GbE)
__Does NOT work__: ASIX (drops connection when VD opens), RTL8157/8159 (5/10GbE not supported)

blocks.name: Cable length
blocks.value: USB3 is rated to ~3m. Run the long distance as **Ethernet**, keep the USB-C short, or use a fiber link cable. Avoid slim/flat USB-C cables over 2m.

blocks.text: Cable Matters USB-C to 2.5GbE Adapter (100W PD)
The canonical wired-Ethernet pick. Realtek 2.5GbE confirmed working on the headset, with 100W passthrough charging.

blocks.name: Info
blocks.value:
__Chipset__: Realtek (2.5GbE)
__Charging__: 100W PD
__Verdict__: Recommended

blocks.text: BENFEI USB-C to Gigabit Ethernet (PD)
The most-vouched adapter in the community thread (multiple users running it daily for months). Realtek RTL8153. May need a USB-C flip for full speed. ~$15-20.

blocks.text: Other Working Adapters
These are confirmed working by community members but with fewer reports than the top picks.

blocks.name: Cable Matters USB-C to Ethernet (140W Charging)
blocks.value: Recommended. Flawless on Q3, keeps the headset charged (~$24). [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0F8TDL49F)

blocks.name: Cable Matters / generic 2.5GbE (PD)
blocks.value: Recommended. Works great at 2.5GbE - you may need to force 2.5G in Windows + router. The 5GbE variant is not supported. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0FC61NLVD)

blocks.name: Thzzhnno USB-C Ethernet
blocks.value: Works - perfect 1Gbps on Q3, but cannot charge at the same time. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0DDKPSNBQ)

blocks.name: BENFEI Secure-Clamp (bring your own cable)
blocks.value: Recommended. Cheapest option that lets you use your own USB-C cable; charges via hub. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0F9WYWT47)

blocks.name: UGREEN CM648 / CM848 / CM275
blocks.value: Confirmed working (Realtek). UGREEN models 25052 / 35921 / 70446.

blocks.name: AXAGON ADE-TXPD
blocks.value: Confirmed working on Q3.

blocks.name: Hoco UA37 (RJ45 to Type-C)
blocks.value: Probably OK - limited reports.

blocks.name: Fazn Type-C to 1000Mbps (PD)
blocks.value: Works (used daily for sim racing). Regional - Greece (Plaisio, plaisio.gr).

pages.name: cables
pages.title: USB-C & Fiber Cables
pages.description: Cables to pair with your adapter

blocks.text: USB-C & Fiber Cables

Run the long distance as **Ethernet** and keep the USB-C between adapter and headset short, or use a fiber link cable for long runs. USB3 is rated to ~3m; 5m+ copper breaks spec. Avoid slim/flat cables over 2m.

blocks.name: Kuject 16ft USB-C Link Cable
blocks.value: Highly recommended - the most-recommended link cable, especially paired with the MyGatti adapter. (See also the /linkcables command.)

blocks.name: Meta / Oculus Official Link Cable (fiber)
blocks.value: Works great - high-quality fiber optic, but expensive.

blocks.name: Cheaper fiber options
blocks.value: Insignia fiber (~$15 on eBay, built like the Meta cable), plus 'onn' (Walmart) and CableCreations fiber.

blocks.name: Confirmed-working USB-C cables
blocks.value: All tested full-speed: [B09FDWG61C](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B09FDWG61C) / [B09VKY632R](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B09VKY632R) / [B0BRCMGJS3](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0BRCMGJS3) (the black one is thinner/more flexible).

blocks.name: USB-C cable (worked without flipping)
blocks.value: Worked right away with several adapters. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0DLNW1T8S)

blocks.name: Kiwidesign L1 Plus charging link cable
blocks.value: Works, great quality.

blocks.text: Couplers, Chargers & Switches
Optional extras to clean up or extend your wired setup.

blocks.name: USB-C female-to-female coupler
blocks.value: Keeps the dongle off the headset - pair with a long USB-C extension. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0BRCQ2G1B)

blocks.name: 65W USB-C power brick
blocks.value: Meta says 18W minimum / 45W for fast charge; 45W is the common sweet spot. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B087MD5MYH)

blocks.name: Cheap 2.5Gb switch
blocks.value: ~$30 4-port 2.5GbE switch - more stable than Internet Connection Sharing. TP-Link / NETGEAR gigabit switches also work.

blocks.text: Connect the Adapter Directly to Your PC (ICS)

You can plug the adapter straight into your PC (no router needed) using Windows Internet Connection Sharing. Enable ICS and follow the persistence step (set the service to automatic + the registry key) so it survives reboots. Alternatively run a standalone DHCP server, or use a cheap 2.5Gb switch for more stability.

blocks.name: Guide
blocks.value: [Crayphish vrdocs (ICS setup, Step 5)](https://github.com/Crayphish/vrdocs)
